title: Loneliness EEG - Roving Oddball Task
description: This dataset comprises 64-channel EEG recordings from 99 young adults performing a roving oddball task with emotional face stimuli. Participants were stratified by UCLA Loneliness Scale scores to investigate whether subjective loneliness is associated with altered automatic processing of social-emotional information, as indexed by event-related potential components elicited by emotional facial expressions. The dataset includes behavioral ratings of stimuli on valence, arousal, and dominance dimensions, along with comprehensive demographic and psychological questionnaire data.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a BioSemi ActiveTwo system (64 channels, 2048 Hz sampling rate) with CMS active electrode reference at Cz and DRL ground at Fz. Participants performed a visual roving oddball task with face stimuli displaying happy or angry expressions, selected from the FACES database and preprocessed using webmorphR and SHINE. Following the EEG session, participants rated face stimuli on three Self-Assessment Manikin dimensions (valence, arousal, dominance) on 1–5 Likert scales.
